title: acaena pinnatifida subsp. aculeata
intro: Acaena pinnatifida subsp. aculeata, also known as the prickly caterpillar bush, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and astringent, which can help to reduce swelling and soothe skin irritations. The health benefits of Acaena pinnatifida subsp. aculeata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or decoctions made from its leaves and stems, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
